title: Psychotherapist — Definition
text:
(a) A person authorized to practice medicine in any state or nation who devotes a substantial portion of their time to the practice of psychiatry.
(b) A person licensed as a psychologist.
(c) A person licensed as a clinical social worker when engaged in applied psychotherapy of a nonmedical nature.
(d) A person serving as a school psychologist.
(e) A person licensed as a marriage and family therapist.
(f)-(o) Various registered associates, interns, and trainees under supervision of licensed professionals.

source.editorial_note: Amended by Stats. 2021, Ch. 647, Sec. 74. Effective January 1, 2022.
